Title: MemorySmith.Agent — Recovery & Error Handling System
Content:
The agent has 4+ recovery paths with different triggers and behaviors. All are implemented in AgentBackgroundService.cs.

## 1. Game Error Recovery (TryRecoverFromGameErrorAsync, line ~1806)

Trigger: ErrorEvent or BlockNotFoundEvent routed to _gameErrors Channel<string>.

Behavior:
- Reads error from _gameErrors channel
- Checks rate-limiting guard (_lastRecoveredGoalName != _currentGoal?.Name — prevents repeated recovery for same goal)
- If not rate-limited: logs recovery, clears _currentGoal, queues GetStatus action
- Waits 1s for status, then replans
- If rate-limited: logs warning, waits for next cycle

## 2. Damage Interrupt (TryInterruptOnDamageAsync, line ~641)

Trigger: ProcessEventsAsync synthesizes DamageTakenEvent from consecutive HealthEvent deltas.

Behavior:
- Compares health delta to DamageInterruptThresholdHp (default 6, per-goal override possible, 0=never)
- If health < threshold: atomically clears action queue, enqueues GetStatus
- Rate-limited by 3s cooldown (_lastInterruptTime) to prevent thrash during combat
- After status: replan via normal dispatch cycle
- On goal completion from damage interrupt: sends 'Safe now.' chat message

Algorithm (line ~641-725):
1. Maintain _firstLowHealthTime — set when health first drops below threshold
2. If health stays below threshold for LowHealthDurationMs (default 3000ms), trigger interrupt
3. This prevents false triggers from single damage spikes

## 3. Stall Auto-Recovery (ReplanGovernor + DispatchActionsAsync)

Trigger: ReplanGovernor detects three identical plan fingerprints with zero inventory delta → STALLED state.

Behavior:
- Three identical plan fingerprints with no inventory change → STALLED
- During STALL: 10s delay, no PlanAsync (avoids tight replan loop)
- Auto-recovery after 60s (STALLED state auto-clears)
- Sprint 40: graduated retry [10, 20, 30, 60] seconds instead of single 60s timeout
- Chat notification on stall detected

## 4. Consecutive Failure Abandonment

Trigger: _consecutiveFailures (incremented on tool call failure) exceeds MaxConsecutiveFailures (default 5).

Behavior:
- Abandons _currentGoal (sets FailureReason, nulls _currentGoal)
- Enqueues GetStatus to get fresh world state
- Rate-limited by _lastAbandonedGoalName == _currentGoal?.Name — prevents loop
- On next dispatch cycle: goal is null → no planning until new goal set

## 5. Timeout Sweep (SweepTimedOutActions, line ~1507)

Trigger: Periodic check in settle phase of DispatchActionsAsync.

Behavior:
- Iterates _correlatedActions.Values
- Finds entries with Status=Dispatched and age > ActionTimeoutSeconds (default 30s)
- CAS-transitions to TimedOut
- If PendingAction was the _actionDispatchedThisCycle action: sets _actionDispatchedThisCycle = false

## 6. Reconnection Recovery (ExecuteAsync, line ~270)

Trigger: WebSocket disconnect, KickedEvent.

Behavior:
- KickedEvent cancels _connectionCts → force full reconnect
- Max 5 reconnection attempts with exponential backoff [2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 32s]
- On reconnect: _currentGoal is preserved → agent resumes current goal
- After max attempts exhausted: sets _connectionStatus = 'disconnected', logs AgentStopped

## Conversation Recovery Prompt

When recovery triggers (damage interrupt, stall, game error), the agent may send a chat message explaining the situation. The message varies by recovery type:
- Damage interrupt: 'I took damage, checking my status...' then 'Safe now.'
- Stall: Chat notification with stall duration
- Game error: 'Something went wrong, recovering...'

## Guards and Rate-Limiting

- _lastRecoveredGoalName: prevents repeated game-error recovery for same goal
- _lastAbandonedGoalName: prevents repeated goal abandonment
- _lastInterruptTime: 3s cooldown on damage interrupt
- _firstLowHealthTime: 3s debounce on low health detection
